title: Weekly Insights Review
summary:
Read here everything you need to know


Latest Trends
To achieve 2030 national climate goals, 700 low-emission and 400 zero-emission ships will be required in Norway alone, according to Norwegian maritime organizations and the shipping industry. Read More

Macro/Geopolitics,Commodity and Freight Markets
The US inflation data for October that was released last week proved to be softer than expected. VLCC earnings have been remarkably strong over the past few months despite the prospect of OPEC+ production cuts which are largely focused on the Middle East.

children.summary:

children.title: Snapshot of Spot Freight Rates, Supply-Demand Trends, Port Congestions
section_path: Snapshot of Spot Freight Rates, Supply-Demand Trends, Port Congestions
children.section_path_text: Snapshot of Spot Freight Rates, Supply-Demand Trends, Port Congestions
children.summary:
The third week of November is characterized by a continued weaker trend in freight rates and demand for smaller vessel categories, while there are signs of stronger momentum in Capesize. Read More
Image reference: unsplash-image-eHlGKM8lpug.jpg | assets/2022-11-20_weekly-insights-review_img_unsplash-image-ehlgkm8lpug_8f0482d596ce.jpg
Image reference: unsplash-image-E-Zl0aLRQK8.jpg | assets/2022-11-20_weekly-insights-review_img_unsplash-image-e-zl0alrqk8_ad429b6cc0b4.jpg

children.title: Russia is already having to travel further to export its crude
section_path: Russia is already having to travel further to export its crude
children.section_path_text: Russia is already having to travel further to export its crude
children.summary: Russian crude exports are having to travel farther as the country redirects flows to "friendly" nations. In this insight, we assess the implications for Russia's shipping logistics, and hence the feasibility of this reshuffle.

children.title: Sentiment across markets was boosted by further signs of easing inflationary pressures
section_path: Sentiment across markets was boosted by further signs of easing inflationary pressures
children.section_path_text: Sentiment across markets was boosted by further signs of easing inflationary pressures
children.summary: Commodities gained as the subsequent weaker USD supported investor appetite in the sector.

children.title: Rising expectations
children.summary:
Despite recent moves by the Chinese authorities to ease some of its Covid policies and provide support for the country's beleaguered property sector, rising infection rates put pressure on the Chinese demand outlook for many commodities. Read More
Image reference: unsplash-image-zLcV9nXr2y0.jpg | assets/2022-11-20_weekly-insights-review_img_unsplash-image-zlcv9nxr2y0_627c5947b2ee.jpg
Image reference: unsplash-image-CQbaooHFSWQ.jpg | assets/2022-11-20_weekly-insights-review_img_unsplash-image-cqbaoohfswq_ad3fe4477f9d.jpg

children.title: Latest global grain export forecasts
children.summary:
The United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) recently released their latest global grain export forecast for the 2022/23 season and has again reduced its expectations. Read More
Image reference: unsplash-image-RuaAlw4BvY8 (2).jpg | assets/2022-11-20_weekly-insights-review_img_unsplash-image-ruaalw4bvy828229_8d07df461a38.jpg

children.title: Cost of COVID Acceptance
children.summary:
The world has moved on from the coronavirus pandemic - except for China. Read More
Image reference: unsplash-image-ZdzO5PgYObA.jpg | assets/2022-11-20_weekly-insights-review_img_unsplash-image-zdzo5pgyoba_525fb9781a09.jpg

children.title: Electric Currents
children.summary: Electric vehicles (EVs) can rightfully claim to be a leading technology to reduce emissions.
